sources.name: ISW Iran Update May 12 2026
ISW Iran Update April 21 2026 — Vahidi-Ghalibaf disagreement
EuroNews Iran IRGC power shift April 22 2026
Critical Threats Evening Special May 12 2026

finding_cards.finding: No Vahidi direct named statement this cycle. ISW (April 21) documented a 'major disagreement' between Vahidi (opposing negotiations) and Ghalibaf (favoring); ISW (May 12) confirmed IRGC consolidating under Vahidi and inner circle — Vahidi is winning the internal power competition. Single-source report (no confirmed outlet, no date) attributes to Vahidi: 'The supreme leader isn't even buried yet, and yet Qalibaf is already shaking hands with those who killed him' — applied -50% discount pending corroboration. Triangle internal dynamics now partially visible: Ghalibaf-Araghchi track is pro-deal; Vahidi apex track appears anti-deal.

discounts_applied: -50% on attributed Vahidi quote (single-source, no confirmed outlet, undated)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: Day 76 read Ghalibaf X post + Jafari + Hassanzadeh as 'Iran-side PA-gap closing.' These are Ghalibaf-faction and mid-tier IRGC, not Vahidi. ISW identifies Vahidi as 'current decision maker' — his opposition (if confirmed) renders Day 76 'closing' imprecise at the apex level that controls ratification.

sources.name: White House readout Trump-Xi summit May 14 2026
CNBC Bessent interview May 14 2026
CBS News live updates Trump-Xi Hormuz May 14 2026
Times of Israel White House readout Hormuz May 14 2026
India TV News Trump-Xi key takeaways May 14 2026

finding_cards.finding: Trump-Xi summit Day 1 produced a substantive joint statement: Hormuz 'must remain open,' Iran 'can never have a nuclear weapon,' Xi will not supply military equipment to Iran, Xi offered to help make a deal, Xi explicitly opposed militarization of Hormuz and any toll regime on its use. Bessent (CNBC, tier-1 adjacent): China 'will be working behind the scenes... to help reopen the Strait.' Xi expressed interest in purchasing US oil to reduce Hormuz dependence. This is the most substantive Chinese commitment-device endorsement since conflict began. Trump's pre-summit 'don't need any help with Iran' (Day 76 read as principal demotion) is confirmed Slantchev-inverse feigning — Xi's price was lowered; Xi delivered.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: Trump-Xi summit communique Iran-specific content — Chinese commitment-device endorsement or absence; Fork B-via-summit dispositive
trigger_detail.framework_variable: Fork B-via-summit recovery channel; Chinese pressure on Iran operationalized; Hormuz toll-regime hysteresis variable
trigger_detail.direction: up — substantive endorsement confirmed; not generic process language; Bessent operationalizes Chinese pressure

variable_moved.variable: Fork B-via-summit recovery channel (30d); Fork B 30d overall
variable_moved.prior_value: Fork B-via-summit 15-25% (Day 76 demoted by Trump pre-arrival framing); Fork B overall 18-26%
variable_moved.new_value: Fork B-via-summit 30-40%; Fork B overall 25-35% (compounding with PROBE-12' 5th-round scheduled). Xi anti-toll language directly targets §9.3 Hormuz-toll hysteresis variable — Chinese pressure now operationalized against that lock-in.
variable_moved.driver: White House readout (tier-1) + Bessent CNBC interview (tier-1); Slantchev pre-demotion confirmed as negotiating posture not genuine principal demotion

finding_cards.conflict_notes: Al Jazeera frames Chinese Hormuz demand as pressure on Iran; White House frames it as joint statement. Both analytically consistent. Xi's Taiwan warning to Trump ('mishandling will cause clashes and conflicts') is in tension with the cooperative Iran frame — Xi extracted a price (Taiwan restraint signal) for the Iran delivery. This is not discounted but noted as the exchange architecture.

sources.name: IDNFinancials Eisenhower final prep May 13 2026
War Zone carrier positions May 11 2026
Army Recognition Eisenhower sea trials

finding_cards.finding: No Eisenhower deployment order issued this cycle. Eisenhower remains on US East Coast in final-prep posture; Navy has not announced next mission. War Zone (May 11) reports 'two carriers' enforcing blockade — potential discrepancy with Day 76 three-CSG claim; likely reflects carrier rotation (Ford departed v3.0 baseline) rather than structural change. No new ROE change, no Dark Eagle deployment, no DOE/SOF nuclear-expertise forward deployment. CENTCOM posture unchanged; 4th-CSG option real but unordered.
finding_cards.conflict_notes: War Zone 'two carriers' vs. Day 76 'three CSG': likely Ford rotation (v3.0 baseline notes Ford departed). If correct, two-CSG operative posture is a subtle restraint signal vs. Day 76 assumption. Flag for PROBE-7 next cycle clarification.

sources.name: Fortune oil price May 14 2026
CNBC WTI/Brent Iran war May 12 2026
CBS News live updates Trump-Xi Hormuz May 14 2026

finding_cards.finding: Brent $107.82 at 0830 ET May 14, down $3.05 from prior morning. Mild downward pressure on summit-day deal-pricing; no decisive breakout below $100. Counter-pressure: 'Iranian export shipments recently stalled' per reporting — first sustained export interruption since conflict start, adding physical tightness. Brent remains within Day 76 $102-110 oscillation band. CBS News live update references 'seized ship taken toward Iran' during summit day — unverified maritime incident; could be Iranian seizure of commercial vessel or coalition enforcement action. Requires follow-up next cycle.

trigger_detail.appendix_b_row: Brent breakout above $115 (Fork A repricing) or below $95 (Fork B credibility); commercial Hormuz restoration announcement
trigger_detail.direction: no change — mid-band; mild deal-pricing on summit, offset by export stall
trigger_detail.gap_to_full_match: Sustained Brent move outside $100-112 band; or commercial Hormuz restoration announcement (Hapag-Lloyd/Maersk equivalent)

variable_moved.new_value: $107.82 on summit day; slight downward; no structural band change. Export stall physical tightness is a new supply factor not in prior model.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: CBS ship-seizure reference unverified. If Iranian seizure of commercial vessel confirmed: Brent would spike and trigger PROBE-8 fork-signaling framework revision. Monitor Day 78.

sources.name: CNN US intelligence — multiple US officials (May 2026)
Times of Israel — Israel readying to strike Iranian nuclear sites if talks fail
Newsweek — Israel prepares strike on Iran amid US tensions
Times of Israel — Knesset dissolution bill submitted May 13 2026

finding_cards.finding: US intelligence (CNN, multiple US officials): Israel is readying to strike Iranian nuclear facilities — air munitions movements observed, air exercise completed, senior Israeli officials' public and private messaging, intercepted Israeli communications. Probability of strike 'has gone up significantly.' Officials caution no final decision confirmed; 'deep disagreement within US government about likelihood.' Strike more likely if US reaches a deal not requiring full uranium removal. Knesset dissolution bill submitted May 13 by coalition; preliminary vote not before May 20; passage all but assured (coalition + opposition both support); election ≤mid-October at earliest. Electoral compression elevates Netanyahu's incentive to demonstrate war-achievement before voters.

discounts_applied: -50% on 'final decision' component (US-official-only sourcing; no Israeli official confirmation; US government internally divided on likelihood)
finding_cards.conflict_notes: CNN intelligence report may partially overlap with May 2025 pre-war warnings; Times of Israel 2026 'readying to quickly strike if talks fail' framing is current. Treating as M confidence given source-dating ambiguity and no Israeli official confirmation.

sources.name: The Hill Senate WPR 7th vote May 13 2026
Time Magazine Senate WPR closest vote yet May 13 2026
CBS News Senate Iran war powers May 13 2026
WaPo Senate rejects Iran WPR May 13 2026

finding_cards.finding: Day 76 7th WPR vote confirmed at 49-50 against. Three Republicans yes (Murkowski, Collins, Paul). Critically: all four Day 76 'in-play' senators (Tillis, Hawley, Young, Curtis) voted NO. Fetterman (D-PA) voted with Republicans — his cross-party vote produced the 49-50 margin. Without Fetterman, resolution would have passed 50-49. Fetterman is now the operative stabilizing variable for Trump, not Republican cohesion. 8th vote not yet scheduled. This structurally revises the Day 76 mechanism: passage path is through Fetterman returning to Democratic caucus position (likely under base pressure) or any one named Republican flipping (less likely after all four held once).

variable_moved.new_value: 60-70% (held); mechanism revised: Fetterman is operative swing. Fetterman under significant base pressure as Democrat voting with Republicans on war powers. Tillis/Hawley/Young/Curtis path to flip harder after all four held once.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: None. Roll-call data is multi-source H confidence.

finding_cards.finding: Oman's Foreign Ministry officially announced the date for the next round of US-Iran talks: Rome, May 23. This interrupts the multi-cycle Meta-Negotiation Regression (Day 76 finding). Venue shift to Rome from Oman may indicate E3 involvement or a neutral technical setting. May 23 falls before the Hajj (~May 25) deadline, compressing the convergence window to 11 days: Trump-Xi communique (May 14-15) → 5th round Rome (May 23) → Hajj (May 25). Note: source may partially conflate with the 2025 pre-war 5th Rome round; treated as 2026 based on Oman FM search context and current-negotiation framing.

finding_cards.finding: No new Trump statement on Iran post-summit Day 1 content yet (summit ongoing). Structural revision required on Iranian-side PA-gap: ISW (April 21) documented Vahidi explicitly opposing negotiations while Ghalibaf favors — the Day 76 'Iran-side PA-gap closing' read was Ghalibaf-faction and mid-tier IRGC speech, not Vahidi apex endorsement. ISW (May 12) confirms Vahidi is winning the internal power competition. Iranian ratification requires Vahidi; Vahidi appears to oppose deal terms. The asymmetric PA-gap is therefore asymmetric in a different dimension than Day 76 modeled: US oscillates on terms; Iran is locked but the locked position at the apex is opposition (Vahidi), not support.

finding_cards.finding: IRGC Tehran 5-day drill codenamed 'Martyred Leader Imam Khamenei' confirmed: anti-helicopter warfare, urban combat readiness, rapid-response, Basij integration. ISW/EuroNews (May 12-13): Iran 'preparing for renewed conflict; military assets remain largely intact; significant missile stockpiles.' Mahshahr coastal drills ~100km from Kuwait's Bubiyan Island confirmed. Multi-source convergence sustains Slantchev feigning-weakness at H confidence. Per ISW, Iranian asset repositioning is making target visibility window finite for Israeli pre-emption planning — this is a structural compressor on Israeli strike timing.

sources.name: CNN US intelligence Israeli preparations
White House readout Trump-Xi May 14 2026
Times of Israel Netanyahu alarm at Trump deal
ISW Iran consolidation Vahidi May 12 2026

finding_cards.finding: Three-vertex asymmetry sharpens post-summit. Israeli dispositional reading: US intel on operational preparations signals Israeli decision-makers are reading Trump-Xi deal-direction as closing window for unilateral action (Powell shifting-power); alarm escalating to operational preparation. Iranian dispositional reading: IRGC drill named 'Martyred Leader Imam Khamenei' + Vahidi opposition to negotiations = maximum dispositional framing stable. US dispositional reading: Trump summit position appears deal-leaning (Xi delivered substantive commitment-device; no contradictory Trump statement yet); A1 oscillation means 48h window applies post-summit. If Trump holds deal-preference post-summit, Israeli pre-emption incentive rises sharply — the more credible Fork B becomes, the more urgently Israel must pre-empt it.

variable_moved.new_value: 28-38% elevated spoiler risk; PROBE-15 and PROBE-9 compounding; Powell mechanism now operative: Trump post-summit deal-lean directly activates Israeli closing-window logic. Fork B strengthening (PROBE-6, PROBE-12') and Israeli spoiler risk rising are not contradictory — they are the framework's race condition.

finding_cards.conflict_notes: The PROBE-6 (Xi delivery strengthens Fork B) and PROBE-15 (Israeli pre-emption incentive rising) findings are structurally coupled, not contradictory: the more credible Fork B becomes, the stronger the Israeli Powell-mechanism pressure to pre-empt before it closes.

sources.name: composite: PROBE-2, PROBE-6, PROBE-9, PROBE-10, PROBE-12', PROBE-13, PROBE-14, PROBE-15

finding_cards.finding: Joint threshold distribution revised since Day 76. (1) Iranian coalition rupture: no signal; Vahidi-Ghalibaf split is new structural visibility, not rupture. (2) Trump A1-binding decision: summit direction deal-leaning; 48h window applies; post-summit Trump statement is the key test. (3) Israeli operational graduation: moved from alarm to readying — most materially changed threshold this cycle; US intel on air munitions movements and exercise completion. (4) WPR-as-forcing-function: Fetterman is operative swing (all 4 named GOP held); 8th vote not scheduled. (5) Strangulation transmission: no new signal. Net: Israeli first-mover threshold is the most advanced. Race condition: Fork B window (summit → 5th round Rome May 23 → Hajj May 25, 11 days) vs Israeli preparation window (operational readying per US intel, potentially days-to-weeks). Framework cannot determine which fires first within 11-day window.

Race condition in 11-day window is the operative analytical object.

Both are real and operating in the same 11-day window. The framework surfaces the race condition; it cannot resolve which fires first.

proposed_revision.edit: Fork B 30-day: 18-26% (Day 76) → 25-35%. Fork D' 30-day: 25-30% → 20-25%. Fork B-via-summit recovery channel: 15-25% (Day 76 demoted) → 30-40%. Xi delivered substantive joint statement: Hormuz must remain open; Iran never nukes; no military equipment; offered to help make deal; Bessent confirms behind-the-scenes Chinese pressure. §5.8: Beijing re-elevated to co-primary mediator with Pakistan-Oman dyad. §9.3: Xi anti-toll language directly targets Hormuz-toll-regime hysteresis variable — Chinese pressure now operationalized against that lock-in.

proposed_revision.edit: 5th round officially scheduled: Rome, May 23 (Oman FM tier-1; provisional — confirm Day 78). Focal point structure: Trump-Xi communique (May 14-15) → 5th round Rome (May 23) → Hajj (May 25) = three compression points in 11 days. Fork D' reduced from 25-30% to 20-25%. BS-11: from multi-cycle meta-deferral as default to active-but-contested. Combined with PROBE-6, Fork B 30-day revised to 25-35%. Flag: May 23 provisional — if source conflates 2025 Rome round, Meta-Negotiation Regression status reverts.

proposed_revision.edit: Israeli unilateral strike (14-21d): 22-32% (Day 76) → 28-38%. Signal moved from diplomatic alarm to operational preparation (US intel on air munitions movements, completed air exercise, intercepted communications). Knesset dissolution election ≤mid-October compresses Netanyahu decision window. Per Powell shifting-power: the more credible Fork B becomes post-summit, the stronger Israeli incentive to pre-empt before deal closes. trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: WPR vote mechanism revised — Fetterman as operative swing variable

proposed_revision.section: BS-5 US Domestic; §5.3 Constitutional Corrosion; §8.1 Constitutional crisis probability
proposed_revision.edit: WPR passage mechanism revised: Day 76 named Tillis/Hawley/Young/Curtis as in-play defectors; Day 76 7th-vote roll call shows all four held. Fetterman (D-PA) voted no with Republicans — his cross-party vote produced the 49-50 margin. Without Fetterman: 50-49 FOR the resolution. Fetterman is the operative stabilizing variable for Trump, not Republican cohesion. Constitutional crisis 30-day probability held at 60-70%; mechanism updated. Next WPR passage: Fetterman returns to Dem caucus, or any one Republican flips. Fetterman under significant base pressure — arguably easier path to passage than prior Republican-defection model.

trigger_digest.appendix_b_row: Iranian-side PA-gap revised larger — Vahidi apex opposes deal; Day 76 'closing' imprecise

proposed_revision.edit: Day 76 'Iran-side PA-gap closing through public principal speech' requires revision at apex level. Ghalibaf-faction (X post) and Araghchi-agent are pro-deal track; Vahidi (ISW-confirmed apex principal, 'current decision maker') per ISW April 21 explicitly opposed to negotiations. Vahidi is winning the internal power competition (ISW May 12). Fork B ratification requires Vahidi; Vahidi appears to oppose. The asymmetric PA-gap is therefore: US oscillates on terms (volatile, no audience cost lock-in); Iran's Ghalibaf-Araghchi track supports deal but Vahidi apex does not — ratification gap is Vahidi-shaped. Provisional pending Vahidi confirmed direct statement (L confidence single-source; see PROBE-2).
